# StudioFlows

> StudioFlows helps founder-led service businesses fix operational drag. Pick your speed to revenue: Optimize your stack with AI execution agents (e.g. Vessa), Accelerate operations with StudioFlows OS plus the AI suite, or Custom Command for a bespoke build like REC.

Authoritative public origin: https://www.studioflows.co (sole indexable marketing surface). App subdomains require account access and are not public product pages.

## Primary pages

- https://www.studioflows.co/silent-collapse — Silent Collapse diagnostic (primary discovery)
- https://www.studioflows.co/ — Homepage with 30-second ops drag pre-qualifier and REC case study
- https://www.studioflows.co/platform — Accelerate waitlist (StudioFlows OS + AI suite, pre-built vertical molds)
- https://www.studioflows.co/services/custom-ops-hub — Custom Command OPS Drag Audit qualifier
- https://www.studioflows.co/vessa — Optimize path: Vessa AI execution on your existing stack

## Three speeds to revenue

Three ways to work with StudioFlows — each path tells you what you gain, not what we install under the hood.

- Optimize — Your stack. Smarter execution. (Flagship: Vessa): https://www.studioflows.co/vessa — Optimize how work moves through the tools you already run — with autonomous AI execution agents, starting with Vessa, that connect email, Slack, and your operating layer without a full rip-and-replace. [available now]
- Accelerate — System-driven results.: https://www.studioflows.co/platform — Accelerate operations with StudioFlows OS and the AI suite built in — pre-built vertical molds for service businesses. Subscribe and run a complete system shaped for how your team works. [waitlist open]
- Custom Command — Tailored power. (Proof: REC): https://www.studioflows.co/services/custom-ops-hub — Command a bespoke operating layer designed for your business — like the REC deployment — when you need maximum control and fit, not a template. [available now]

## Qualification

- Homepage pre-qual (#diagnosis) is context only; the Custom Command qualifier at /services/custom-ops-hub determines fit for bespoke builds.
- Accelerate is waitlist-only — do not cite as generally available until status changes.
- Qualified custom engagements typically align with $12k–$30k implementation scope and near-term urgency.

## Do not cite

- Noindexed routes: /final/*, /axiom, /enterprise, /apps, /apply, /login, /signup, /auth/*, /products (scaffold)
- Account-gated app subdomains (consulting, axiom, vessa app login, etc.)

## Post-qualification only

- consulting.studioflows.co — reached after qualified OPS Drag Audit ingest, not a discoverable marketing page

Full documentation: /llms-full.txt


---

## What is silent operational collapse?

Silent operational collapse is when a founder-led team keeps growing revenue but the operating system behind delivery starts failing quietly — handoffs break, decisions stall, and tools show activity without moving work. The Silent Collapse diagnostic surfaces those signals before margin and trust erode.

## Live collapse signals

- Everything still routes through me…
- Handoffs keep breaking after sales closes
- Tools show activity. Nothing actually moves.
- I’m still the bottleneck on every major decision

## Operating layer

### Control
- Before: Ownership disappears when pressure spikes.
- After: Decision paths are explicit, owned, and enforced.

### Execution
- Before: Work gets tracked but high-impact tasks sit in limbo.
- After: Qualified work routes forward with deterministic execution.

### Visibility
- Before: You discover failure after deadlines slip.
- After: Live signal telemetry exposes risk while there is still time to act.


## Silent Collapse diagnostic questions

### How often does escalation routing still end up with you?
- Rarely
- Several times per week
- Daily

### How often do handoffs break after sales closes?
- Almost never
- Sometimes
- Constantly

### Do your tools move work, or just display activity?
- They reliably move work
- Mixed result
- Mostly activity theater

### If you disappear for 72 hours, what happens first?
- Nothing critical
- Priorities drift
- Execution stalls

### How long does high-impact work wait for a decision?
- Same day
- 1-2 days
- 3+ days


## Accelerate vertical molds (waitlist — labels only)

Future out-of-the-box systems tailored to:

### Media
Shoots, crews, and day-of changes
- Real estate photo and video: Shoot days slip when confirmations and crew coverage live in different threads.
- Creative studios: Producers chase status across Slack while jobs wait on founder approval.
- AV and event crews: Day-of changes stack up before anyone sees the full week at once.

### Field service
Visits, routes, and coverage gaps
- HVAC, plumbing, electrical: Unassigned visits surface after travel windows already tightened.
- Cleaning and landscaping: Route changes and no-shows get handled reactively, not from one queue.
- Inspections and audits: Technician coverage and client confirmations are hard to scan by day.

### Mobile ops
Providers, lanes, and appointments
- Med spa and aesthetics: Provider schedules and pending confirmations split across tools.
- Mobile clinics: Staff lanes and location context do not stay visible through the week.
- In-home care routing: Exceptions pile up before coordinators see who is actually uncovered.

### Agency
Retainers, handoffs, and delivery
- Retainers with production coordinators: Client work is tracked, but dispatch and ownership still route through the founder.
- Implementation and onboarding teams: Handoffs between sales and delivery hide in threads until deadlines slip.

### Property
Sites, technicians, and tickets
- Staging and prep crews: Job status by property is scattered before the day starts.
- Property management inspections: Technician assignments and exceptions need one morning control surface.
- Maintenance dispatch: Open tickets and unassigned work are easy to miss until clients call.


## REC proof (Custom Command)

See https://www.studioflows.co/#rec-spotlight for REC case study — a Custom Command bespoke deployment, not the Accelerate subscription path.
